entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Dual-purpose sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) used for grain and stover

entities.name: NUTRITIVE VALUE
entities.description: The quality of sorghum stover as a feed source for livestock

entities.name: IN VITRO GAS PRODUCTION
entities.description: A measure of digestibility (Gas48hr) used to assess nutritive value

entities.name: NEUTRAL DETERGENT FIBRE
entities.description: A measure of cell wall components (NDF) in sorghum leaves and stems

entities.name: ACID DETERGENT FIBRE
entities.description: A measure of cellulose and lignin (ADF) in sorghum leaves and stems

entities.name: LIGNIN
entities.description: A complex organic polymer that affects the digestibility of sorghum stover

entities.name: NITROGEN CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen present in sorghum leaves and stems

entities.name: ASH CONTENT
entities.description: The inorganic residue remaining after the combustion of sorghum leaves and stems

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A research study conducted in the field to evaluate sorghum cultivars under various conditions

entities.name: MULTI-ENVIRONMENT TESTING
entities.description: A method of evaluating genotypes across multiple environments to account for GE interactions

entities.name: SEMIARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region where sorghum crop residue is extensively used for livestock feed

entities.name: FERTILITY REGIMES
entities.description: Different levels of soil nutrients applied during the field experiment

entities.name: PLANT DENSITY REGIMES
entities.description: Different spacing or population densities of sorghum plants in the field

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of nitrogen to the soil to affect crop growth and quality

entities.name: YEAR
entities.description: The temporal factor (1995 and 1996) during which the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals that utilize sorghum crop residue as a feed source

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: Differences in traits among sorghum genotypes

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL VARIATION
entities.description: Differences in traits caused by varying environmental conditions

entities.name: GENOTYPE BY ENVIRONMENT (GE) INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The interaction between a genotype and its environment affecting trait expression

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: The Indian state where the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research was carried out

relationships.description: Sorghum stover is evaluated for its nutritive value
Nutritive value is important for livestock feed
Sorghum is evaluated for in vitro gas production
Sorghum is evaluated for neutral detergent fibre content
Sorghum is evaluated for acid detergent fibre content
Sorghum is evaluated for lignin content
Sorghum is evaluated for nitrogen content
Sorghum is evaluated for ash content
Field experiment used to evaluate sorghum cultivars
Field experiment tested different fertility regimes
Field experiment tested different plant density regimes
Field experiment conducted in Andhra Pradesh
Andhra Pradesh is located in India
Genetic variation affects the nutritive value of sorghum
Environmental variation affects the nutritive value of sorghum
In vitro gas production exhibited substantial GE interactions
Multi-environment testing is needed to address GE interactions
Nitrogen fertilization had little influence on in vitro gas production
Plant density regimes had little influence on in vitro gas production
Year had little influence on in vitro gas production
Sorghum is extensively used in the semiarid tropics
